preamble: The relationship between alveolar fricatives and lateral fricatives is a feature of many Athabaskan languages. Shifts from [s] to [ɬ] often occur as part of complex consonant mutations or historical shifts in the family.

examples.note: Navajo exhibits complex morphophonemic alternations involving sibilants and lateral fricatives.

sources: Mithun, M. (1999). The Languages of Native North America.
Young, R. W., & Morgan, W. (1987). The Navajo Language.
